criteria: local differential elements with uscdi-requirement and without uscdiplusquality
exclusionRules: exclude if local element type.profile or type.targetProfile references http://fhir.org/guides/onc/us-quality-core/StructureDefinition/
exclude if only diff is uscore mustSupport=true while local mustSupport is missing

local.description: Identifies what "kind" of plan this is to support differentiation between multiple co-existing plans; e.g. "Home health", "psychiatric", "asthma", "disease management", etc.

diffs.local: There may be multiple axes of categorization and one plan may serve multiple purposes.  In some cases, this may be redundant with references to CarePlan.concern.

local.description: Codes describing events that can trigger the initiation of a goal.

diffs.local: The date or event after which the goal should begin being pursued.
Goals can be established prior to there being an intention to start pursuing them; e.g. Goals for post-surgical recovery established prior to surgery.
not-done
The reason the service was not requested
extensible
http://fhir.org/guides/onc/us-quality-core/ValueSet/us-quality-core-negation-reason

diffs.uscore: When the product was given out. For mailed prescriptions, when the label was printed

diffs.local: The set of RxNorm codes to represent medications
(USCDI)
diffs.uscore: (USCDI) How the medication is to be used by the patient or administered by the caregiver

uscore.description: A code specifying the state of the prescribing event. Describes the lifecycle of the prescription.

diffs.local: preferred
http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medication-codes

local.description: Codes for high level observation categories.

diffs.local: In addition to the required category valueset, this element allows various categorization schemes based on the owner’s definition of the category and effectively multiple categories can be used at once.  The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set.
A code that classifies the general type of observation being made.
Used for filtering what observations are retrieved and displayed.
(USCDI) Classification of type of observation

In addition to the required category valueset, this element allows various categorization schemes based on the owner’s definition of the category and effectively multiple categories can be used at once.  The level of granularity is defined by the category concepts in the value set.

Used for filtering what observations are retrieved and displayed.

local.description: Codes specifying why the result (`Observation.value[x]`) is missing.

diffs.local: Provides a reason why the expected value in the element Observation.value[x] is missing.
For many results it is necessary to handle exceptional values in measurements.
